macro_segments.title: The Source of the Omer: Proximity to Jerusalem and the Principle of Not Passing Over Mitzvot

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah: Mitzvat ha-Omer to bring from nearby grain to Jerusalem
Explanation of 'Karmel' as soft and full grain; Two rationales for bringing from nearby (Karmel quality vs. Ein Maavirin)
Extended discussion of Ein Maavirin principle with examples from Tefillin practice

macro_segments.title: The Hashmonean Siege and the Exceptional Sourcing of Omer and Shtei Halechem

micro_segments.title: Historical context: Horkanous and Aristobolus in conflict; daily money-for-sacrifices exchange
The traitorous elder using Greek wisdom to communicate with besiegers; the pig sacrifice as violation of Antiochus decrees
The land shakes; curses pronounced against pig-raising and teaching Greek wisdom
Connection to Megillat Taanit and the burned fields explaining need for distant grain sources
The deaf-mute's gestural communication identifying Gagot Tserifim for Omer sourcing
Second deaf-mute's gestural communication identifying Ein Socher for Shtei Halechem sourcing
Analysis: Parallel between traitor's misuse and Mordechai's proper use of communication methods

macro_segments.title: Mordechai's Linguistic Wisdom and the Three Women's Bird Offerings

micro_segments.title: Three women bringing bird offerings with unclear indications of their status (zavah, oceanic flow, period)
Mordechai's reinterpretation: the women's references indicate health threats, not tumah; their offerings are olot
Connection to Mishnah on Petachiah; identification with Balshan; his ability to mix languages and interpret creatively
Sanhedrin members' knowledge of seventy languages; Mordechai's special ability to blend and interpret

macro_segments.title: The Procedure for Harvesting the Omer on Erev Yom Tov and Yom Tov

micro_segments.title: Agents of Beit Din mark sheaves on Erev Yom Tov; entire region gathers for public harvesting
Elaborate questioning and triple affirmation of sunset, tools, and harvesting; public consensus regarding the day
Framing of procedure against Baytusin opposition regarding harvesting on Yom Tov vs. Sunday after Shabbat

macro_segments.title: The Tamid Offering Debate: Individual vs. Communal Sponsorship

micro_segments.title: Source from Megillat Taanit: establishment of proper Tamid practice and Shavuot
Tzedukim position: individual can donate and bring Tamid based on singular language in pasuk
Pharisaic response: 'Et Korbani' uses collective language; communal funds required for Tamid
Analysis: Tzedukim attempt to break from communal reality vs. Pharisaic preservation of consensus

macro_segments.title: The Shavuot Debate: Atzeret After Shabbat vs. After Yom Tov

micro_segments.title: Baytusin position: Shavuot falls on Sunday (Atzeret after Shabbat); establishment of Shavuot as holiday
Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai's dismissive tone calling Baytusin 'idiots'; refusal to engage substantively
Elder's counter-argument: Moshe Rabbeinu loved Israel and ensured two days of Shavuot by placing it on Sunday
Ben Zakkai's harsh rebuttal using wilderness wandering proof; refusal to engage with weak argument
Assessment: debate appears to hinge on tradition (masora) vs. peshat; rhetorical silencing of opposition

macro_segments.title: Textual Proofs for Shavuot's Date: Fifty Days and Seven Weeks

micro_segments.title: Two verses: 'Count fifty days' vs. 'Seven full weeks'; reconciliation through conditional interpretation
When Yom Tov falls on Shabbat, full weeks are possible; when on weekday, only day count applies
Tosafot's treatment of why 50 days if only 49 are counted; Rosh's 'rounding' solution

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Proof: Singular 'Count for You' Indicates Dependence on Beit Din and Month Establishment

micro_segments.title: Singular vs. plural forms of 'count': individual counting vs. Beit Din's role in month establishment
Exclusion of regular Shabbat; dependence on Yom Tov and month establishment by Beit Din
Conclusion: counting depends on Pesach determination, not on weekly Shabbat cycle

topical_tags.term: Ein Ma'avirin al Hamitzvot (not passing over mitzvot)
Pig (chazir) sacrifice as violation of Antiochus decrees
Ein Socher (place of Shtei Halechem sourcing)
Mimchrat HaShabbat (day after Shabbat) for Omer counting
Sefira tluya b'veit din (counting dependent on Beit Din)
Yom Tov (holiday) vs. Shabbat for Omer determination
